summary: iter-5 qualitative review clean across all 4 presets x 6 scenes; second consecutive pass — convergence reached

issues.details: Stem swatches in the right sidebar (Vocals pink, Piano warm amber, Other purple) inherit the warm Classic Dark hues via the JS spread in presets.js; they read as category tags but introduce a faint warm-vs-cool tension against Midnight's cool blue accent and surfaces. Not blocking — stem identity should remain stable across themes for muscle memory — but flagged for the ship report.

Stem note fills on the piano roll appear muted on the cream surface — the --alpha-stem-fill token (0.85) was tuned for light hues over near-black and carries over verbatim. Notes remain readable but lack the punch they have in Classic Dark. Optional follow-up: per-theme override (e.g. 0.95 in Studio Light) if a future polish pass wants more bite.

Topbar minimap segments render as solid amber blocks at HC's --alpha-overlay-strong=0.85, which is louder than the subtle outline they read as in the dark themes. This is consistent with HC's stated AAA/saturation goal but worth recording as an intentional design choice rather than a regression.

notes: Reviewer iter-5: axe scan returned 0 findings; qualitative sweep across 24 screenshots returned 3 minor notes and 0 major/blocker issues. All 4 presets render coherently across the 6 scenes — typography rhythm is consistent, modal scrim, picker selection, sidebar tabs, transport bar and lyrics list all hold together within each preset. Studio Light passes the 'real working light theme, not a dumping ground' bar. Midnight + High Contrast canvas elements (piano roll, minimap, function bar) inherit theme correctly rather than looking like Classic Dark embedded in another preset. With iter-4 already passing, this is the second consecutive pass — the loop's convergence criterion is satisfied.
